title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
After discovering that Croce was the one injured by nail holes in the palms and feet, Wain and Escham return to Arroyo. Wain contacts District Attorney Krümmit, who promises to keep their unauthorized handling of a corpse secret and arranges for Captain Pickert and a coroner to arrive. The pair then travels toward Stowberville, Ohio.

On Tuesday, a multi-state pursuit involving cars, trains, and planes begins. Ellery Queen arrives in Stowberville and receives a note from Yardeley at the Ford Stowberville Hotel. The note reveals that a man matching a specific description checked in at midnight and left by taxi at 7:30 a.m. heading for Chinsville; the man had a bandage on his wrist and had abandoned a limp. Ellery identifies this man as the killer and contacts the Chief of Police in Cheswick, using the alias Ellery Quincannon to request assistance.

Ellery travels to the Clayton Inn in Cheswick, where Police Chief Hardy informs him that Yardeley’s contact reported the suspect changed his route to Columbus instead of Chinsville. At the Seneca Hotel in Columbus, Ellery finds another note from Yardeley stating that the suspect, referred to as "K," took a 1:00 p.m. train to Indianapolis. Yardeley intends to catch a plane to intercept him. After learning that a plane has already been rented for him at the airport, Ellery flies toward Indianapolis. Meanwhile, Wain and Escham arrive in Columbus and fly to Indianapolis via a plane arranged from Chillicothe. As they travel, Ellery reflects on the murder of Andrew Van, feeling confident in his progress.
